body.u: I left school in the summer of 1979 to work in an office as a secretary it was a big place is London and I didn’t know many people, after I didn’t know many people, after about a year and a half I left to work for a bigger and better company where I could earn more money as I was planning to marry within the next year or two and we had planned to save to buy our own house near his parents. After about 6 months the engagement broke up and I didn’t get over it for ages. I thought I would change my job, I would go in for nursing, I would learn a profession and find other interests. In the next five years I had gained A husband, a family and a house of our own is a nice part of London. When my two children started school I started nursing again at a hospital again at a hospital nearby. But it got too tiring with the children as well. So I decided to pack it in. When I was in my late twenties my husband was offered a job is Saudi Arabia. He went on his own, and sent money back for me and the kids. The kids grew rapidly and hardly saw their daddy. When my husband eventually come home the children were leaving school and going out to work. They were twin girl and boy. The girl worked in a Bank and the boy joined the navy. My mother was really good to me while my husband was away so we bought a holiday of a lifetime on a cruise around the meditteraneanMediterranean. But on her return we sadly lost her it took me two years to get over that. But when I went back to nursing. We moved house down to a little cottage in Kent nearer home. It was a lovely place and we never left it for long because everything in it belonged to us and we had worked hard for it. When I was 55 a grandmother to 5 my husband passed away through a serious operation.

I still had our family though and my grandchildren were lovely 2 girls and three boys. I family emigrated to Australia and the others lived nearby. They asked me if I wanted to go but I said that I was nearly at the age of retirement and the travelling would be too much for me. Many other things happened in my life but these were the most important to me. I suffer from Arthuritusarthritis now very badly but itsit'stooto be expected when you get old.
